lines.target: It was a bright cold day in April, and the clocks were striking thirteen.
lines.base: Era un día claro y frío de Abril y los relojes marcaban las trece horas. (2)

lines.target: Winston Smith, his chin nuzzled into his breast in an effort to escape the wind,
lines.base: Winston Smith, con el mentón hundido en su pecho en un esfuerzo para protegerse del horroroso viento, (3) (4)

lines.target: slipped quickly through the glass doors of Victory Mansions, though not quite quickly enough
lines.base: pasaba rápidamente por las puertas de cristal de Victory Mansions, aunque no lo suficientemente rápido

lines.target: to prevent a swirl of gritty dust from entering along with him.
lines.base: para evitar que un torbellino de polvo arenoso entrara junto con él.

lines.target: The hallway smelt of boiled cabbage and of old rag mats. At one end of it a poster, too large for indoor display,
lines.base: El entrada olía a col hervida y a viejos tapices de trapo. Y en un extremo un «poster» a color, demasiado grande para ser colocado en el interior,

lines.target: was tacked to the wall. It depicted simply an enormous face, more than a metre wide: the face of a man of about forty-five,
lines.base: había sido clavado en la pared. (5)

lines.target: with a heavy black moustache and some features. Winston made for the stairs.
lines.base: Mostraba simplemente un rostro enorme, de más de un metro de ancho: el rostro de un hombre de alrededor de cuarenta y cinco años de edad,

lines.target: It was no use trying the lift. Ever at its best it was seldom working,
lines.base: con un bigote negro y espeso y de rasgos atractivos. Winston se dirigió a las escaleras. (6)

lines.target: and at present the electric current was cut off during daylight hours.
lines.base: Es inútil intentarlo por el ascensor. Aún en el mejor de los casos, funciona raramente,

lines.target: It was part of the economy drive in preparation for Hate Week.
lines.base: y en el presente, la corriente eléctrica estuvo cortada durante las horas diurnas.

lines.target: The flat was seven flights up, and Winston, who had a varicose ulcer above his right ankle,
lines.base: Era parte de un esfuerzo económico para preparar la Semana del Odio. (7)

lines.target: went slowly, resting several times on the way.
lines.base: El apartamento está en el séptimo piso, y Winston, que tenía treinta y nueve años y tenía una úlcera varicosa encima de su tobillo derecho,

lines.target: On each landing, opposite the lift-shaft, the poster with the enormous face gazed from the wall.
lines.base: subió lentamente, reposando varias veces en el camino.

lines.target: It was one of those pictures which are so contrived that the eyes follow you about when you move.
lines.base: En cada descanso, frente a la cabina del ascensor, el «poster» con un rostro enorme vigilando toda la pared.

lines.target: The caption beneath it ran:
lines.base: Era una de esas imágenes que son diseñadas de tal manera que los ojos le siguen a uno cuando se mueve frente a ellas.

lines.target: BIG BROTHER IS WATCHING YOU
lines.base: El pie de foto proclamaba:

lines.target: It was London. He tried to squeeze out some childhood memory that should tell him whether London had always been quite like this.
lines.base: ¡EL GRAN HERMANO TE VIGILA!

lines.target: The Ministry of Truth was startlingly different from any other object in view.
lines.base: Era Londres. El intentó desterrar de su mente un recuerdo de la niñez que le diría si Londres había sido siempre así. (8)

lines.target: It was an enormous pyramid of glittering white concrete, soaring up, 300 metres into the air.
lines.base: El Ministerio de la Verdad era sorprendentemente diferente de cualquier otro objeto a la vista.

lines.target: From where Winston stood it was just possible to read, picked out on its face in elegant lettering,
lines.base: Era una enorme estructura piramidal de hormigón blanco y brillante, que se elevaba hasta 300 metros en el aire.

lines.target: the three slogans of the Party:
lines.base: Desde donde Winston se encontraba era justamente posible leer, destacado en letras elegantes

lines.target: WAR IS PEACE
lines.base: en su fachada, los tres lemas del partido:

lines.target: FREEDOM IS SLAVERY
lines.base: LA GUERRA ES LA PAZ

lines.target: IGNORANCE IS STRENGTH.
lines.base: LA LIBERTAD ES ESCLAVITUD
LA IGNORANCIA ES FORTALEZA. (9)

gap_candidates.target: It was a bright cold day in April, and the clocks were striking thirteen. (2)
Winston Smith, his chin nuzzled into his breast in an effort to escape the wind, (3) (4)
slipped quickly through the glass doors of Victory Mansions, though not quite quickly enough
to prevent a swirl of gritty dust from entering along with him.
The hallway smelt of boiled cabbage and of old rag mats. At one end of it a poster, too large for indoor display,
was tacked to the wall. (5) It depicted simply an enormous face, more than a metre wide: the face of a man of about forty-five,
with a heavy black moustache and some features. Winston made for the stairs. (6)
It was no use trying the lift. Ever at its best it was seldom working,
and at present the electric current was cut off during daylight hours.
It was part of the economy drive in preparation for Hate Week. (7)
The flat was seven flights up, and Winston, who had a varicose ulcer above his right ankle,
went slowly, resting several times on the way.
On each landing, opposite the lift-shaft, the poster with the enormous face gazed from the wall.
It was one of those pictures which are so contrived that the eyes follow you about when you move.
The caption beneath it ran:
BIG BROTHER IS WATCHING YOU
It was London. He tried to squeeze out some childhood memory that should tell him whether London had always been quite like this. (8)
The Ministry of Truth was startlingly different from any other object in view.
It was an enormous pyramid of glittering white concrete, soaring up, 300 metres into the air.
From where Winston stood it was just possible to read, picked out on its face in elegant lettering,
the three slogans of the Party:
WAR IS PEACE
FREEDOM IS SLAVERY
IGNORANCE IS STRENGTH. (9)
